issues.code:
2 from datetime import datetime
3 from xml.etree.ElementTree import ElementTree, XMLParser
4 from jwt import decode as jwt_decode
issues.description: Using ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
issues.judge_reason: Same root cause as B314. The import of ElementTree itself is not the vulnerability; the usage matters. The code does use a parser with resolve_entities=False, which reduces risk but does not eliminate all XML attack vectors. Marking UNCERTAIN for consistency with B314.

issues.code:
13     # Read XML payload from request body
14     xml = ElementTree(XMLParser(resolve_entities=False)).fromstring(request.data)
15
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.XMLParser to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.XMLParser with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: The code sets resolve_entities=False, which disables external entity resolution. However, ElementTree may still be vulnerable to other XML attacks like billion laughs (entity expansion) because resolve_entities=False only prevents external entity references, not internal entity expansion. Without defusedxml, there is residual risk. Marking UNCERTAIN because the explicit resolve_entities=False mitigates the main XXE vector but not all XML bombs.
